title: Review Swarm: render-fixture — branch feature/login-retry
summary_bullets: Adds retry logic to the login handler with a configurable backoff.
Retry loop can spin hot when backoff is zero — flagged by both reviewers.

verdict.rationale: Two HIGH findings (one convergent) in the retry path; fix the hot loop and the swallowed error before merging.

reviewers.name: sre
reviewers.takeaway: Retry loop with zero backoff will hammer the auth backend during an outage.

reviewers.name: vasco
reviewers.takeaway: Solid structure; error handling hides config mistakes & uses bare except.

findings.body: Retry loop has no lower bound on backoff: if `backoff <= 0` the loop spins hot and DoSes the auth backend during an outage.
findings.fix: Clamp backoff to a minimum (e.g. `max(backoff, 0.1)`) and cap total attempts.

findings.body: Bare `except Exception` around the auth call hides programming errors as retryable failures.
findings.fix: Catch the specific transport error (`AuthTimeout`) and let everything else propagate.

findings.body: `int(raw)` on the backoff env var raises ValueError which is swallowed by the caller — misconfiguration silently falls back to defaults.
findings.fix: Log a warning with the rejected value before falling back.

findings.body: No tests cover the new retry behavior — neither the backoff clamp nor the give-up path.
findings.fix: Add a unit test that simulates two failures then success, and one that exhausts retries.
